{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01EY29aQuEYNX5GfwaSBa8kE","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# PRD 1: Prisma setup and schema migration\n     2→\n     3→max_turns: 30\n     4→\n     5→---\n     6→\n     7→## Context\n     8→\n     9→The backend is being migrated incrementally from Express + TypeORM to NestJS + DDD + CQRS + Prisma.\n    10→This PRD introduces Prisma as the new ORM and migrates the existing TypeORM schema to `prisma/schema.prisma`.\n    11→\n    12→TypeORM remains in place and continues to run — this PRD does NOT remove TypeORM or touch any existing\n    13→legacy code. Prisma is added alongside TypeORM so new modules can use it immediately.\n    14→\n    15→Already migrated modules for reference: `src/modules/health/`, `src/modules/version/`.\n    16→\n    17→---\n    18→\n    19→## Goal\n    20→\n    21→Install Prisma, create `prisma/schema.prisma` that mirrors the existing database schema exactly,\n    22→and generate the Prisma client — without modifying any existing TypeORM entity, migration, or runtime code.\n    23→\n    24→---\n    25→\n    26→## Bounded context\n    27→\n    28→This PRD is infrastructure-only. No new `src/modules/` bounded context is created.\n    29→\n    30→---\n    31→\n    32→## Requirements\n    33→\n    34→### 1. Install Prisma\n    35→\n    36→```bash\n    37→npm install prisma --save-dev\n    38→npm install @prisma/client\n    39→npx prisma init --datasource-provider postgresql\n    40→```\n    41→\n    42→This creates:\n    43→- `prisma/schema.prisma`\n    44→- `.env` update with `DATABASE_URL` (do not commit real credentials)\n    45→\n    46→### 2. Schema — mirror the existing database exactly\n    47→\n    48→The schema must reflect the current live database. Do NOT change table names, column names,\n    49→or types — Prisma must connect to the existing Supabase/PostgreSQL database without any migration.\n    50→\n    51→```prisma\n    52→generator client {\n    53→  provider = \"prisma-client-js\"\n    54→}\n    55→\n    56→datasource db {\n    57→  provider = \"postgresql\"\n    58→  url      = env(\"DATABASE_URL\")\n    59→}\n    60→\n    61→model User {\n    62→  id           Int       @id @default(autoincrement())\n    63→  username     String    @unique @db.VarChar(255)\n    64→  email        String    @unique @db.VarChar(255)\n    65→  password     String\n    66→  salt         String\n    67→  sessionToken String?\n    68→\n    69→  eventsCreated AppEvent[] @relation(\"EventCreatedBy\")\n    70→  eventsAdmin   AppEvent[] @relation(\"EventAdmins\")\n    71→\n    72→  @@map(\"users\")\n    73→}\n    74→\n    75→model AppEvent {\n    76→  id               Int       @id @default(autoincrement())\n    77→  code             String    @unique\n    78→  name             String\n    79→  createdAt        DateTime  @default(now())\n    80→  expiresAt        DateTime?\n    81→  updatedAt        DateTime  @updatedAt\n    82→  arePartiesVisible Boolean  @default(false)\n    83→\n    84→  createdById Int\n    85→  createdBy   User @relation(\"EventCreatedBy\", fields: [createdById], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n    86→\n    87→  characters Character[]\n    88→  admins     User[]      @relation(\"EventAdmins\")\n    89→\n    90→  @@map(\"events\")\n    91→}\n    92→\n    93→model Character {\n    94→  id               Int     @id @default(autoincrement())\n    95→  name             String\n    96→  characterClass   String\n    97→  specialization   String\n    98→  iLevel           Int\n    99→  role             String\n   100→  bloodLust        Boolean @default(false)\n   101→  battleRez        Boolean @default(false)\n   102→  keystoneMinLevel Int     @default(2)\n   103→  keystoneMaxLevel Int     @default(99)\n   104→\n   105→  eventCode String?\n   106→  event     AppEvent? @relation(fields: [eventCode], references: [code], onDelete: Cascade)\n   107→\n   108→  @@map(\"character\")\n   109→}\n   110→\n   111→model EventAdmin {\n   112→  eventId Int @map(\"event_id\")\n   113→  userId  Int @map(\"user_id\")\n   114→\n   115→  @@id([eventId, userId])\n   116→  @@map(\"event_admins\")\n   117→}\n   118→```\n   119→\n   120→### 3. Do NOT run prisma migrate\n   121→\n   122→The database already exists and is managed by TypeORM migrations.\n   123→Use `prisma db pull` to verify the schema matches, then `prisma generate` to generate the client.\n   124→\n   125→```bash\n   126→npx prisma generate\n   127→```\n   128→\n   129→Do NOT run `prisma migrate dev` or `prisma db push` — this would conflict with TypeORM migrations.\n   130→\n   131→### 4. Add DATABASE_URL to environment config\n   132→\n   133→Add to `.env.example`:\n   134→```\n   135→DATABASE_URL=postgresql://USER:PASSWORD@HOST:PORT/DATABASE\n   136→```\n   137→\n   138→Add to the CI workflow env vars if needed (use existing POSTGRES_* vars to construct it).\n   139→\n   140→### 5. Verify Prisma client works\n   141→\n   142→Create a minimal smoke test at `src/config/prisma.ts`:\n   143→\n   144→```typescript\n   145→import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';\n   146→\n   147→const prisma = new PrismaClient();\n   148→export default prisma;\n   149→```\n   150→\n   151→This shared client will be used by all future infrastructure repositories.\n   152→\n   153→---\n   154→\n   155→## What this PRD does NOT include\n   156→\n   157→- No removal of TypeORM — it stays in place\n   158→- No new NestJS module\n   159→- No new HTTP endpoints\n   160→- No changes to existing entities, routes, controllers, or services\n   161→- No `prisma migrate dev` or `prisma db push`\n   162→- No data seeding\n   163→- No changes to existing tests\n   164→\n   165→---\n   166→\n   167→## Acceptance criteria\n   168→\n   169→- [ ] `prisma/schema.prisma` exists and mirrors the existing database schema exactly\n   170→- [ ] `@prisma/client` is installed and `npx prisma generate` runs without error\n   171→- [ ] `src/config/prisma.ts` exports a shared PrismaClient instance\n   172→- [ ] `.env.example` includes `DATABASE_URL`\n   173→- [ ] `npm run build` passes\n   174→- [ ] `npm run lint` passes\n   175→- [ ] `npm run test` passes — no regression on existing unit tests\n   176→- [ ] TypeORM entities, migrations, and runtime are untouched\n   177→- [ ] No existing routes, controllers, or services are modified\n   178→- [ ] `.github/`, `infra/`, `secrets/`, `docker-compose.yml` are untouched\n   179→\n   180→---\n   181→\n   182→## Notes for Claude Code\n   183→\n   184→- The database already exists in production (Supabase/PostgreSQL) — do NOT run any migration command\n   185→- `prisma db pull` can be used to introspect the schema but requires a live DB connection in CI —\n   186→  instead, write the schema manually from the TypeORM entities described in this PRD\n   187→- The `Party` class in `src/entities/party.entity.ts` is NOT a TypeORM entity (no `@Entity` decorator)\n   188→  and has no database table — do NOT add it to the Prisma schema\n   189→- Table names to use exactly: `users`, `events`, `character`, `event_admins`\n   190→- The join table `event_admins` has columns `event_id` and `user_id`\n   191→- Character joins Event via `eventCode` → `Event.code` (not via `Event.id`)\n   192→- TypeORM enums (CharacterClass, Role, Specialization) are stored as strings in the DB —\n   193→  use `String` in Prisma, not Prisma enums, to avoid conflicts with existing data\n   194→- Do not add `@@relation` names that conflict with existing foreign key constraint names in the DB\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. If it does, you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer high-level questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d56eed33-fa2a-4534-be5a-3bb50bcedd18","uuid":"347bff48-a5cd-4dbc-ae62-05454533428a"}
